title: Today's AI Brief: Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?
summary: Microsoft's Build developer conference and OpenAI's Codex update make waves in AI
imageAlt: Image from source article for Has Microsoft Lost Its Mojo (Again)?

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI briefing. This week, Microsoft's annual Build developer conference kicked off with a keynote from Satya Nadella. The focus was on agentic AI - think of it like AI that can act on its own, making decisions and taking actions. It's an area where Microsoft is clearly trying to make a big splash.

According to Wired, Nadella's speech was your typical boastful affair, with plenty of fanfare around new products and AI capabilities. But what's interesting here is that Microsoft seems to be playing catch-up in the agentic AI space. It's a crowded field, with plenty of other players vying for attention. Has Microsoft lost its mojo... again?

Meanwhile, OpenAI has been making waves with its Codex tool, which is being positioned as a productivity powerhouse for knowledge work. The idea is that Codex can help with research, data analysis, workflow automation, and even content creation. It's an interesting play, and one that could have significant implications for how we work.

And it's not just Microsoft and OpenAI - the AI landscape is rapidly evolving, with new players and technologies emerging all the time. It's a thrilling space to watch, and I'm excited to see what the future holds.

Codex is becoming a productivity tool for everyone is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: The Next Era of Knowledge Work report explores how Codex is transforming productivity through AI-powered research, data analysis, workflow automation, and content creation. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Take our I/O 2026 quiz, vibe coded in Google AI Studio. is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Heads up, builders and devs: You can test your knowledge of our I/O 2026 announcements with this vibe coded quiz built in Google AI Studio. In case you missed it, at Google I/O 2026 , we announced new ways and places you can use Google AI Studio . With Google AI Studio, powered by the Antigravity coding agent, you can bring ideas to life with the latest Gem... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
